identity.name: Solution
identity.definition: A Solution is a bundle of Renaissance products sold as a unit, serving as the primary commercial offering under Renaissance Intelligence.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a Solution appears in product management dashboards and catalog grids

attributes.name: Name
attributes.description: Display name of the Solution bundle.

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state: active, inactive, or deprecated.

representations.heading: List layout
representations.description: How a Solution appears in management tables and assignment workflows

attributes.name: Name
attributes.description: Display name of the Solution bundle.

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state: active, inactive, or deprecated.

representations.heading: Detail page layout
representations.description: What users see when they open a full Solution record

attributes.name: Solution ID
attributes.description: Unique system identifier for this Solution.

attributes.name: Name
attributes.description: Display name of the Solution bundle.

attributes.name: Apps Tag
attributes.description: Machine-readable tag used in the apps platform for routing and identification.

attributes.name: Activation Logic
attributes.description: Rules that determine how the Solution is activated for a school or district.

attributes.name: Override Rules
attributes.description: Exceptions to standard activation logic for specific populations.

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state: active, inactive, or deprecated.

stories.title: View Solution Catalog
stories.benefit: I can understand which product bundles are available and what each includes.
stories.whenClause: I navigate to the Solution catalog,
stories.thenClause: I see a list of all Solutions with their included product counts and current status.

stories.title: Review Included Products
stories.benefit: I can verify the correct products are bundled before assigning the Solution to a school.
stories.whenClause: I open a Solution's detail page,
stories.thenClause: I see the full list of included Products with their tiers and availability.

businessRules.title: Uniqueness
businessRules.description: A Solution is uniquely identified by its Solution ID. The Apps Tag must also be unique across all Solutions.

businessRules.title: Product Membership
businessRules.description: A Solution must include at least one Product. Products can belong to multiple Solutions simultaneously.

businessRules.title: Status Transitions
businessRules.description: Active Solutions can be set to inactive (temporarily unavailable) or deprecated (permanently retired). Deprecated Solutions cannot be reactivated.

businessRules.title: Assignment Impact
businessRules.description: Changing a Solution's included Products does not retroactively affect existing Product Assignments. Only new assignments pick up the updated bundle.

businessRules.title: Deprecation Cascade
businessRules.description: Deprecating a Solution does not deprecate its included Products. Products remain independently available for direct assignment.

states.name: Active
states.description: Available for assignment to schools and districts
states.triggers: Admin creates or activates the Solution

states.name: Inactive
states.triggers: Admin sets to inactive; existing assignments unaffected

states.name: Deprecated
states.triggers: Admin deprecates; no new assignments allowed

relationships.mechanics: Products are added to a Solution by an Admin during bundle configuration. A Solution defines which Products are included.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. A Solution includes one or more Products; a Product can belong to multiple Solutions.
relationships.sorts: Products within a Solution: alphabetical by name (default), by tier, by product type.
relationships.filters: By tier, by availability status, by subject area, by product type.
relationships.dependencies: Removing a Product from a Solution does not affect existing Product Assignments that were created from the Solution.

allAttributes.name: Solution ID
allAttributes.description: Unique system identifier for this Solution.

allAttributes.name: Name
allAttributes.description: Display name of the Solution bundle. Must be unique across active Solutions.

allAttributes.name: Apps Tag
allAttributes.description: Machine-readable tag used in the apps platform for routing and identification.

allAttributes.name: Included Products
allAttributes.description: Products bundled in this Solution. At least one required.

allAttributes.name: Activation Logic
allAttributes.description: Rules that determine how the Solution is activated for a school or district. Includes entitlement checks and license validation.

allAttributes.name: Override Rules
allAttributes.description: Exceptions to standard activation logic for specific populations, pilot programs, or promotional access.

allAttributes.name: Status
allAttributes.description: Current lifecycle state: active, inactive, or deprecated.

structure.evidence: A Solution has meaningful attributes: name, apps tag, included products, activation logic, override rules, and status. A detail page can be designed around these.

instances.evidence: Renaissance Star 360 (5 products), myON + Accelerated Reader (2 products), Freckle + Lalilo (2 products). Each represents a distinct commercial bundle.

purpose.evidence: Solutions are the primary unit of sale for Renaissance Intelligence. Admins browse Solutions to understand what is available, and Product Assignments reference Solutions to provision access.

sipValidation.verdict: Domain object. The commercial packaging layer that groups Products into assignable bundles.

synonyms.notes: Common in commercial conversations about product packaging
Used in proposals and order forms
Used to describe comprehensive product collections

shapeshifterMatrix.description: Solutions shown as cards with included products and status. Admins browse the catalog to understand product bundles and their availability.
Solutions listed during the provisioning workflow, showing included products and status. Admins select which solution to assign to a school or district.
The complete solution record — ID, included products, activation logic, and override rules. Admins review how a solution is configured and what products it enables.
